nodes.label: Coating Thickness Failure Correlation
nodes.description: Observed pattern: when electrode coating variance exceeds 2.5 microns, formation failure rate jumps from 3% to 11%.
nodes.creator: people-quality-eng

nodes.label: Line 3 Calibration Sweet Spot
nodes.description: Taught by Production Manager: Line 3 calendering roller pressure works best at 142 bar, not the spec 145. 145 causes micro-cracking in NMC cathodes.
nodes.creator: people-prod-mgr

nodes.label: Night Shift Yield Adjustment
nodes.description: Mined from 12 months of data: night shift yield averages 1.8% lower due to humidity fluctuation in the dry room. Compensate by reducing coating speed by 5%.
nodes.creator: people-prod-mgr

questions.question: Line 3 yield dropped 4% this week. Is it the new cathode batch or the calibration change from Tuesday?
questions.answer: The 4% yield drop correlates with Tuesday's calibration change (145 bar), not the cathode batch. The Line 3 Calibration skill identifies that 142 bar is optimal for NMC cathodes - 145 causes micro-cracking. Coating variance at 3.1 microns predicts 11% formation failure. Night shift humidity is ruled out (drop is uniform across shifts). Recommended action: revert to 142 bar immediately and monitor 50 cells.
reasoningSteps: Yield Deviation Investigation triggers: Electrode Coating Line A yield below rolling average by 4%.
Entity traversal: Electrode Coating Line A -> NMC811 Cathode Powder -> current cathode batch.
Skill fires: "Line 3 Calibration Sweet Spot" (Department priority) - checks current roller pressure. Tuesday calibration set it to spec 145 bar. Skill says optimal is 142 bar for NMC cathodes.
Skill fires: "Coating Thickness Failure Correlation" - current coating variance at 3.1 microns (above 2.5 threshold), predicting 11% formation failure.
Skill fires: "Night Shift Yield Adjustment" - night shift contribution checked: yield drop is uniform across shifts, ruling out humidity as primary cause.
Quality Gate Decision triggers: recommend reverting to 142 bar and monitoring next 50 cells.
People routed: Production Manager gets calibration revert recommendation. Quality Engineer gets monitoring protocol.

questions.question: Electrode Coating Line A shows a 4% yield drop this week. Is it a material or process issue?
questions.answer: Line A uses NMC811 Cathode Powder, Graphite Anode Slurry, Aluminum Foil, and Copper Foil. In-Process QC Gate checks electrode weight, thickness, and adhesion per batch, and the Metrology Lab monitors Line A via SEM/XRD analysis. Check if incoming QC flagged any material batch issues - NMC811 Cathode Powder from Umicore and separator films from Celgard both pass through Incoming QC Gate before release to Line A.
reasoningSteps: Electrode Coating Line A uses 4 key materials
In-Process QC checks electrode parameters per batch
Metrology Lab monitors Line A with SEM/XRD
NMC811 Cathode Powder from Umicore passes Incoming QC
Check if incoming material inspections flagged deviations
Correlate yield drop timing with material batch changes

questions.question: Based on electrode data from the last 8 hours, which cells in formation are likely to fail final QC?
questions.answer: Electrode Coating Line A feeds into Cell Assembly Line B, which then feeds Formation & Aging Chamber. Cells produced in the last 8 hours are now in the 14-day aging rack. In-Process QC at Line A tracks electrode weight and thickness - any batches flagged there are at risk of failing Final QC Gate capacity grading. The Cycle Life Testing Lab also tracks formation performance to detect early degradation patterns.
reasoningSteps: Coating Line A feeds Cell Assembly Line B
Cell Assembly feeds Formation & Aging Chamber
In-Process QC tracks electrode parameters at Line A
Flagged batches in formation are at risk of failing Final QC
Cycle Life Testing Lab monitors formation cycling data
Cross-reference in-process flags with formation chamber data

questions.question: We need to increase Prismatic Cell 100Ah production by 20%. Where are the bottlenecks?
questions.answer: Prismatic Cells are produced at Formation & Aging Chamber (14-day aging cycle) and assembled at Module Assembly Line C. The aging chamber is the likely bottleneck - it's a time-based constraint. Both Cylindrical and Prismatic cells share this chamber. Upstream, Cell Assembly Line B handles electrolyte fill from Showa Denko and separator films from Celgard. Incoming QC Gate must handle increased material inspection throughput.
reasoningSteps: Prismatic Cells produced at Formation & Aging Chamber
14-day aging cycle is a time-based bottleneck
Both Cylindrical and Prismatic cells share this chamber
Cell Assembly Line B is upstream - handles electrolyte and separator
Incoming QC must handle increased material inspection volume
Module Assembly Line C downstream needs capacity validation
